description:
`src/worktree.rs:156` (`link_shared_state`) plants a symlink `<worktree>/.balls/local → store.local_dir()` in every claimed worktree. The symlink is convenience inherited from the pre-XDG `.balls/local/<claims|locks|...>/` layout; in XDG mode it points at the Phase 1A catch-all bucket and is the *only* reason that bucket gets walked from worktree-side code.

SPEC §3 makes every per-clone slot directly addressable under `~/.local/state/balls/`:

> `claims/<nested-clone-path>/` — claim files (per clone)
> `locks/<nested-clone-path>/` — task locks (per clone)
> `plugins-auth/<nested-clone-path>/` — plugin tokens, identity (per clone, per user)

Code inside a worktree that needs "this clone's claims/locks/plugin-auth" can resolve those XDG paths via the `Store` it already holds (`Store::discover` finds the main repo root from a worktree; the same accessors apply).

## Scope

1. Audit every site that traverses `<wt>/.balls/local/<x>` (or constructs that path string-wise). Each call gets rewritten to use the appropriate Store accessor (`Store::claims_dir`, `Store::locks_dir`, etc.) which already resolves the XDG slot directly.
2. Delete the `link_state_path(store.local_dir(), &wt_balls.join("local"))` line in `worktree::link_shared_state`. The `state-repo` symlink (SPEC §3-aligned per-clone state-repo checkout) and the `tasks` symlink stay — both are SPEC-named.
3. Update worktree tests that assert `<wt>/.balls/local` exists as a symlink.

## Out of scope

- Deletion of `Store::local_dir()` — parent (bl-a11d).
- The `state-repo` and `tasks` symlinks; they have SPEC homes.
title: Drop per-worktree .balls/local symlink; consumers resolve XDG paths directly
